title: Benchmarking Weakly Supervised Factor-Localized SAEs on Frozen Vision Features
description: This project is a benchmark/protocol paper, not a new SAE-method paper. It tests whether weak supervision through a fixed latent partition yields cleaner held-out factor-local responses than matched SAE baselines on frozen vision features, using exact single-factor counterfactual pairs, factor-preserving nuisance pairs, and leakage-free pseudo-block evaluation.
motivation: Recent papers already cover transformation-aware, equivariant, and stability-oriented sparse autoencoders, so a new method claim would be weak. The real gap is a controlled benchmark that fairly compares partitioned and unpartitioned SAE variants on exact intervention-style tests, with explicit handling of invariant and residual blocks and with guardrails against backbone mismatch.
proposed_approach: Cache frozen features from a primary backbone (DINOv2-small) on dSprites and Shapes3D, plus a small secondary sensitivity backbone (OpenCLIP ViT-B/32) on dSprites only. Fit linear probes first to determine which factors are exposed by each backbone, then train five matched-capacity methods: TopK-SAE, RA-SAE, Orbit-SAE, FB-SAE, and FB-OSAE. Replace the earlier unsound orbit transforms with two independently corrupted views of the same image using only factor-preserving sensor corruptions, and evaluate unpartitioned models with training-split-only pseudo-blocks that explicitly define factor, invariant, and residual blocks before held-out TFCC and TBA are computed.

related_work.title: Group Crosscoders for Mechanistic Analysis of Symmetry
related_work.how_it_differs: This already studies transformation-aware sparse dictionaries. The proposal does not compete as a new symmetry-learning method and instead contributes a narrower benchmark on weak supervision plus fixed partitioning.

related_work.title: Group Equivariance Meets Mechanistic Interpretability: Equivariant Sparse Autoencoders
related_work.how_it_differs: Equivariant SAEs address group structure directly. Here they serve as evidence that methodological novelty is limited, reinforcing the paper's benchmark positioning.

related_work.title: Archetypal SAE: Adaptive and Stable Dictionary Learning for Concept Extraction in Large Vision Models
related_work.authors: Thomas Fel, Ekdeep Singh Lubana, Jacob S. Prince, Matthew Kowal, Victor Boutin, Isabel Papadimitriou, Binxu Wang, Martin Wattenberg, Demba Ba, Talia Konkle
related_work.how_it_differs: RA-SAE is the strong structured baseline in the study. Its inclusion prevents overclaiming gains that only hold against plain SAEs.

related_work.title: beta-VAE: Learning Basic Visual Concepts with a Constrained Variational Framework
related_work.authors: Irina Higgins, Loic Matthey, Arka Pal, Christopher Burgess, Xavier Glorot, Matthew Botvinick, Shakir Mohamed, Alexander Lerchner
related_work.how_it_differs: This motivates the use of dSprites-style controllable-factor data, but it is not a post-hoc interpretability benchmark on frozen pretrained vision features.

related_work.title: Understanding Disentangling in beta-VAE
related_work.authors: Christopher P. Burgess, Irina Higgins, Arka Pal, Loic Matthey, Nick Watters, Guillaume Desjardins, Alexander Lerchner
related_work.how_it_differs: This supports the use of Shapes3D-style factorized evaluation. The proposal borrows the benchmark logic, not the generative-modeling setup.

related_work.title: A Framework for the Quantitative Evaluation of Disentangled Representations
related_work.authors: Cian Eastwood, Christopher K. I. Williams
related_work.how_it_differs: This paper formalizes factor-alignment evaluation. The proposed work adapts that evaluation mindset to SAE counterfactual localization metrics.

related_work.title: Disentangling by Factorising
related_work.how_it_differs: FactorVAE is a classic factorized representation-learning method. The proposal instead studies post-hoc SAE decompositions of frozen pretrained features.

related_work.title: Challenging Common Assumptions in the Unsupervised Learning of Disentangled Representations
related_work.authors: Francesco Locatello, Stefan Bauer, Mario Lucic, Gunnar Raetsch, Sylvain Gelly, Bernhard Schoelkopf, Olivier Bachem
related_work.how_it_differs: This paper is a direct reason to avoid a broad factor-discovery claim and to frame the work as modest weakly supervised benchmarking.

related_work.title: RAVEL: Evaluating Interpretability Methods on Disentangling Language Model Representations
related_work.authors: Jing Huang, Zhengxuan Wu, Christopher Potts, Mor Geva, Atticus Geiger
related_work.how_it_differs: RAVEL provides the core evaluation philosophy: use intervention-style benchmarks instead of qualitative examples. The proposal transfers that philosophy to frozen vision features.

hypothesis: On dataset-backbone pairs where the frozen features expose the ground-truth factors, partitioned weakly supervised SAEs will outperform matched unpartitioned baselines on held-out TFCC and TBA under a leakage-free evaluation protocol. Any additional advantage from nuisance-invariance regularization beyond partitioning alone is a secondary hypothesis rather than the main claim.
success_criteria: The benchmark uses only factor-preserving nuisance pairs and defines invariant, factor, and residual blocks for every model without using validation or test information for block assignment.

On DINOv2-small, at least one partitioned method (FB-SAE or FB-OSAE) improves mean held-out TFCC by at least 0.05 absolute over all three unpartitioned baselines on both dSprites and Shapes3D for probe-admissible factors.
The same partitioned method improves TBA over Orbit-SAE by at least 5 percentage points on both datasets.
FB-OSAE is only credited with added value beyond partitioning if it beats FB-SAE on TFCC or TBA on both datasets while preserving at least 95% of FB-SAE variance explained.
On the one-seed OpenCLIP dSprites sensitivity check, the partitioned-vs-unpartitioned family ranking does not fully reverse.
Under permutation of factor-block identities at evaluation time, partitioned-model TBA falls near the 1/F chance rate.
